title: Smoke/Steam Particles + Loop Celebration

tasks.title: Smoke/Steam CSS Particle System
tasks.description: Add a CSS-based particle system that emits small gray/white smoke puffs from the locomotive smokestack during play. Particles should drift upward, expand slightly, and fade out.
implementation: Add @keyframes smoke-rise animation: translate upward 30-50px, scale from 1 to 1.8, opacity from 0.7 to 0
Add .smoke-particle CSS class with absolute positioning, 3-6px diameter, gray/white background, border-radius 50%
Create spawnSmokeParticle() function that creates a div, positions it at the locomotive's current screen position (smokestack area), adds animation, removes after animation completes
Start a smoke interval in startPlay() that spawns particles at a rate of ~3-5 per second at 1x speed
Scale spawn rate with speed slider: interval = 300ms / speed (so faster train = more smoke)
Maintain a particle pool: max 30 alive particles. If limit reached, remove oldest before spawning new
Stop smoke interval in stopPlay()
Particles must be appended to #grid-container (same parent as animated train) so they stay in grid coordinate space
Add slight random horizontal drift to each particle for natural look (randomize translateX ±10px)
acceptance_criteria: Visible smoke puffs trail from locomotive during play
Smoke rate increases when speed slider is moved right
Max 30 particles in DOM at any time
No performance degradation (CSS-only animations, no JS per frame for particles)
Particles appear near the locomotive's smokestack area and drift upward

tasks.title: Loop Completion Detection
tasks.description: Detect when the train completes a full loop (returns to its starting cell after visiting at least one other cell). Track this in animState.
implementation: Add animState.loopCompleted = false flag in startPlay()
Add animState.cellsVisited = 0 counter
In animateFrame(), when entering a new cell, increment cellsVisited
When new cell matches startRow/startCol and cellsVisited >= 4, set loopCompleted = true
Only trigger celebration once per play session (check !animState.loopCompleted before triggering)
acceptance_criteria: Loop detected when train returns to start after traversing the loop
Does not false-trigger on start (cellsVisited gate)
Only triggers once per play session
Train continues playing after loop detection (doesn't stop)

tasks.title: Confetti Celebration Effect
tasks.description: When a full loop is completed, trigger a confetti burst: 20-30 colored dots burst outward from the train's position and fade. Play a triumphant sound and show a toast.
implementation: Add @keyframes confetti-burst: translate outward in random direction (50-120px), scale from 1 to 0.5, opacity from 1 to 0, with slight gravity (translateY adds extra downward)
Add .confetti-particle CSS class: 6-10px circles, random bright colors, position absolute
Create triggerLoopCelebration() function that spawns 25 confetti particles at train's current position
Each confetti particle gets a random direction (CSS custom properties --dx, --dy for varied directions)
Add SFX.celebrate() sound: ascending arpeggio (C-E-G-C) with a bright timbre
Show toast: '🎉 Full Loop!'
Confetti particles auto-remove after 1.5s animation
Call triggerLoopCelebration() from the loop detection code in T2
acceptance_criteria: Confetti burst visible at train location on loop completion
20-30 colored particles burst outward and fade
Confetti particles are cleaned up after animation
Effect doesn't block or interrupt train animation

testing_focus: Random → Play → watch full loop: smoke visible, celebration triggers on loop completion
Speed slider: smoke rate visibly changes with speed
Stop/restart: smoke cleans up on stop, reappears on restart
No performance issues: check for smooth 60fps with particles active
Multiple loops: celebration only triggers once per play session
Dead end: smoke works until crash, no celebration on non-loop
All existing features still work (drag/drop, save/load, auto-connect, undo)
